algorithms.title: Heat Stroke Response
algorithms.description: Recognition and cool-first management of environmental heat stroke.
steps: Suspect heat stroke in any hot-environment patient with altered mental status.
Confirm severe hyperthermia; move the patient out of the heat.
Begin rapid active cooling immediately — ice-water immersion where feasible, otherwise ice packs to neck/axillae/groin plus evaporative cooling.
Manage airway and breathing; check glucose; avoid antipyretics (ineffective for environmental hyperthermia).
Continue cooling during transport ('cool first, transport cooling'); monitor temperature to avoid overshoot.
During heat events, pair clinical response with outreach to isolated, at-risk residents.

algorithms.title: Post-Storm Carbon Monoxide Screen
algorithms.description: Detecting the silent post-disaster killer in outage conditions.
steps: After storms/outages, ask every symptomatic household about generators, charcoal, or heaters used indoors or in attached garages.
Suspect CO with headache, nausea, dizziness, confusion — especially multiple household members or pets affected.
Remove everyone from the environment; give high-flow oxygen.
Transport for evaluation (consider CO-oximetry); alert responders to the source.
Push prevention messaging: generators outdoors, away from windows, with CO alarms.
